title: Super3D Extra Format Bridge
description:
Super3D Extra Format Bridge

Import and export more than 20 3D file formats that are not natively supported by Blender.

This add-on adds additional items to Blender’s File → Import and File → Export menus, allowing users to open and save a wider range of common 3D formats.

Supported Formats




DCC / Animation

  • Cinema 4D (.c4d)

  • 3ds Max (.max)

  • Maya ASCII (.ma)

  • Maya Binary (.mb)

  • Houdini (.hip / .hiplc / .hipnc)

  • Fusion 360 (.f3d)

CAD / Engineering

  • AutoCAD DWG (.dwg)

  • STEP (.step / .stp)

  • IGES (.iges / .igs)

  • IFC (.ifc)

  • Parasolid X_T (.x_t)

  • JT (.jt)

Other 3D Formats

  • SketchUp (.skp)

  • 3D Studio (.3ds)

  • 3MF (.3mf)

  • APX (.apx)

  • 3DXML (.3dxml)

How It Works


Import

  1. Select a supported file format from File → Import.

  2. The add-on uploads the file to the Super3D conversion service.

  3. The file is converted to OBJ format and imported into the current Blender scene.

Export


  1. Select a supported format from File → Export.

  2. The scene is exported as OBJ.

  3. The OBJ file is converted to the selected format through the Super3D service and saved locally.

Key Features

  • Support for more than 20 common 3D file formats

  • Integration with Blender’s native Import / Export menus

  • Single lightweight add-on file (.py)

  • Compatible with Blender 4.0 and newer

  • Configurable API timeout (30 seconds to 2 hours)

  • Option to keep or automatically remove temporary files

  • No external dependencies other than Blender’s built-in Python

Installation

  1. Download the add-on .zip file.

  2. Open Blender and go to Edit → Preferences → Add-ons.

  3. Click Install… and select the downloaded .zip file.

  4. Enable the add-on in the add-ons list.

Requirements

  • Blender 4.0 or later

  • Internet connection

Built by 475519905
documentation:
Installation

1. Download the product zip file.

2. In Blender, go to Edit > Preferences > Add-ons.

3. Click Install, choose super3d_io_addon.zip, then enable the addon.


Usage

- Import: File > Import > Super3D

- Export: File > Export > Super3D


Best Practices

- Use supported Blender versions listed on the product page.

- For production pipelines, validate exported files before batch deployment.

- Keep naming conventions consistent for predictable results.


Troubleshooting

- If import/export fails, test with a minimal sample file first.

- Disable conflicting addons and retry.

- Include Blender version + OS + sample file when reporting issues.
